note: The original published document from a national authority
Unmodified record as extracted from the source document
Component owning one country's pricing publication regime; parses sources into canonical records and surfaces anomalies, but never reads other countries' data
Normalized, typed price record produced by the Country Delegate
A reading of what a national price field means and how it relates to fields in other countries
Assessment of whether a policy-named field actually exists, is populated, and behaves plausibly in data
A deterministic rule specifying how a derived value is computed from its inputs
A paired price record across two countries, with evidence attached, pending review
Human or agent assessment of a comparison candidate's usability
A finding that something does not fit the system's current assumptions
Price at the manufacturer exit point in the supply chain
Price the payer (national health system or insurer) reimburses
Price at which the pharmacy acquires the product
Price paid by the end consumer at the pharmacy counter
A national price field that does not map cleanly to any comparison vocabulary category
A usability verdict: the two prices cannot be meaningfully compared
WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical classification
International Nonproprietary Name
European Directorate for the Quality of Medicines; publishes Standard Terms for dosage forms, routes, containers
Unified Code for Units of Measure
European Economic Area: 27 EU member states plus Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway
Framework Agreement on the Supply and Pricing of Medicines (Ireland)
Joint pharmaceutical policy initiative: Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg, Austria, Ireland
Generic term for a price reimbursed by a national health system
Price at the wholesale distribution stage
Price at the retail (pharmacy) point of sale
A price set or used by a national authority as a benchmark for reimbursement or clustering
A maximum permissible price set by regulation
A regulatory measure that prevents price increases for a defined period
A statutorily required discount or clawback applied to the list price
